title: ED procedure note documentation prompt
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

referral.text: Supports documentation of consent, site verification, proposed procedure and approach, team communication, checklist use and monitoring requirements.
referral.rationale: The exact RCEM procedure-safety section supports documenting patient concerns, clinician risk context, procedure-related assessment, severity, appearance, clinician plan, specialist discussion, completed consent and post-procedure handover only when applicable; it does not select a procedure.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documentation of consent, site verification, proposed procedure and approach, team communication, checklist use and monitoring requirements.

source.title: Invasive Procedures in the Emergency Department

follow_up.text: Supports documenting consent or the reason consent was impossible, site verification and sedation-proforma use when applicable.
follow_up.rationale: The exact audit section supports recording a completed procedure report and measured or reviewed data only when performed; it does not imply successful completion.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports documenting consent or the reason consent was impossible, site verification and sedation-proforma use when applicable.

source.title: Invasive Procedures in the Emergency Department

sources_used.title: Invasive Procedures in the Emergency Department
sources_used.jurisdiction: United Kingdom emergency medicine; UAE consent, capacity and procedural policy govern local use

source_limitations: Procedure, indication, operator, assistants, site, laterality, consent basis, checklist, preparation, anaesthesia, equipment, technique, findings, specimen, complications, monitoring and aftercare remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
